TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Abyssal Demons Faction.

Translations.Comment: Faction name. Used formal plural form 'Demoni' with descriptive adjective 'Abissali' to match Spanish/French pattern and fantasy tone.
Serpentine demonic creatures. Feminine plural 'Vipere' with consistent 'Abissali' adjective for parallel structure with faction name.
Lesser demonic infantry. Used 'Prole' (brood/offspring) to convey 'spawn' meaning similar to Spanish 'Vástagos' and German 'Brut'.
Disease-bearing demons. Compound form 'Camminatori della Peste' maintains serious wargame tone; literal translation of 'walkers of plague'.
Small plague demons. Created diminutive with '-ini' suffix (Italian diminutive) on 'Peste' root, similar to German 'Seuchenlinge' and Spanish 'Pestillejos'.
Demons wreathed in flames. Used 'Demoni di Fuoco' following Spanish compound pattern for consistency with other demon types.
Flying plague-bearing insects. Feminine plural form 'Mosche' with genitive 'della Peste' parallel to Spanish 'Moscas de la Peste'.
Death-dealing demons. Used 'Mietitori' (reapers) to convey harvesting/death imagery; 'Abissali' maintains consistency with faction naming.
Hellish hunting beasts. Direct compound with masculine plural 'Cani' and adjective 'Demoniaci' for clarity and wargame tone.
Demonic cavalry. Used 'Cavalieri dell'Inferno' (Knights of Hell) with genitive for authoritative, ominous tone matching Spanish pattern.
Massive plague monster. 'Colosso' conveys imposing, titanic presence; genitive 'della Peste' shows plague affiliation.
Greater demon lord. Created with 'Arci-' prefix (Italian for 'arch-') + 'dimone' to convey supreme authority, similar to Spanish 'Archidemonio'.
Powerful demonic ruler. Used singular masculine 'Principe' with adjective 'Abissale' to emphasize noble rank and abyssal origin.
Messenger and champion of the abyss. Formal 'Araldo' with 'Abissale' maintains authoritative tone of a demonic champion messenger.
